Trump practically told our allies to f-off — that we didn’t need them, that the U.S. had already “won.”

Now he’s begging them for help.


ian bremmer (@ianbremmer)

trump, last saturday: don’t need british ships. we will remember, we don’t need people that join wars after we’ve already won.

trump, this saturday: hopefully united kingdom will send ships so that strait of hormuz will no longer be threatened.



— https://nitter.net/ianbremmer/status/2032837592882778613#m

Trump says the U.S. will be “bombing the hell out of the shoreline” and shooting Iranian boats out of the water to keep the Strait of Hormuz open, while claiming Iran’s military capability has been “100% destroyed” — a claim that contradicts known realities on the ground.

He also says he hopes countries like China, France, Japan, South Korea, and the U.K. will send warships to join the U.S. in the effort.
